steps.title: Recognize the slope in the linear function
steps.content: The function $g(m) = -0.05m + 12.1$ is in the form $y = mx + b$, where the slope is $m = -0.05$. In a linear model, the slope represents the rate of change of the output with respect to the input — in this case, the rate of change of remaining gasoline (in gallons) per mile driven.

steps.title: Interpret the slope as consumption rate
steps.content: A slope of $-0.05$ means that for each additional mile, the remaining gasoline decreases by $0.05$ gallons. Since the tank is losing $0.05$ gallons per mile, the car uses $0.05$ gallons per mile. That matches choice A — A is correct.
